content.full_text:
State of California

M e m o r a n d u m

To     : Advice File: Section 84102                Date  : Dec. 27, 1984
                     84211                 /M-84-930
                     84214


From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Carla Wardlow

Subject: Applicability of Elections Code Section 12404--Disposition
        of Surplus Campaign Funds


Elections Code Section 12404 states, in part:

       Upon leaving any elective office, or at the end of
       the post election reporting period following the
       defeat of a candidate for elective office, whether
       occurs last, surplus campaign funds under the con-
       trol of a former candidate or officeholder or his or
       her controlled committee shall only be used or held
       for the following purposes: ....

Recently, I spoke with Gene Hill of the Attorney General's
office and asked him if his office interprets Section 12404
as applying to all surplus campaign funds. He indicated
that because it refers specifically to funds held by office-
holders who are leaving office and defeated candidates, the
restrictions set forth in Section 12404 apply only to those
individuals. He has no problem with suggesting to persons
not covered by the provisions that they may want to use
12404 as a guide when disposing of surplus funds.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Elections Code Section 12404 regarding disposition of surplus campaign funds apply to all candidates and officeholders, or only to those leaving office or defeated candidates?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Section 12404 applies only to officeholders who are leaving office and defeated candidates. For persons not covered by the statute, Section 12404 may be suggested as a guideline for disposing of surplus funds, but is not mandatory.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal FPPC memorandum rather than a traditional advice letter format. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Information extracted from body text containing discussion of Attorney General's interpretation. Relevant code sections cited: 84102, 84211, 84214, and Elections Code 12404.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Elections Code Section 12404 regarding disposition of surplus campaign funds apply to all candidates and officeholders, or only to those leaving office or defeated candidates?

CONCLUSION: Section 12404 applies only to officeholders who are leaving office and defeated candidates. For persons not covered by the statute, Section 12404 may be suggested as a guideline for disposing of surplus funds, but is not mandatory.

Recently, I spoke with Gene Hill of the Attorney General's office and asked him if his office interprets Section 12404 as applying to all surplus campaign funds. He indicated that because it refers specifically to funds held by office- holders who are leaving office and defeated candidates, the restrictions set forth in Section 12404 apply only to those individuals. He has no problem with suggesting to persons not covered by the provisions that they may want to use 12404 as a guide when disposing of surplus funds.
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum documenting consultation with the Attorney General's office regarding the scope of Elections Code Section 12404's restrictions on surplus campaign fund disposition, concluding the statute applies only to specific categories of individuals.
